title: cyperus hyalinus
intro: Cyperus hyalinus, also known as the dwarf papyrus, is a type of sedge plant that belongs to the Cyperaceae family, characterized by its slender stems and delicate white flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Cyperus hyalinus include its potential to aid in the treatment of wounds, fever, and digestive issues, as well as its use as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. Herbal preparations of Cyperus hyalinus are typically made by infusing the plant's leaves or roots in hot water, and can be consumed as a tea or used as a topical treatment.
